name: Chocolate Toffee Icebox Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Confectioners' sugar (plus for sprinkling, optional)
2 packages Vanilla instant pudding mix (3.4 oz each)
4 Heath bars; chopped (about 1-1/4 cups/1.4 oz each)
Edible flowers; optional (such as Johnny Jump-Ups)
recipeInstructions:
Use plastic wrap to line a 9x5x3-inch loaf pan, overhanging sides and ends by 2 inches. Arrange cookies on the bottom of the pan, overlapping slightly; set remaining cookies aside.
In a medium-sized bowl with an electric mixer at high speed, beat 1 cup cream, 1 tablespoon confectioners' sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
In a large bowl with a mixer at medium speed, beat pudding mix and half-and-half until smooth. Using a rubber spatula, gently but thoroughly fold in whipped cream and chopped Heath bars.
Spread one-third of the pudding mixture over cookies in the bottom of the loaf pan; layer 8 reserved cookies on top. Repeat layers twice, using half of the remaining pudding mixture for each, ending with the final 8 cookies on top.
Cover the pan with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight until set. At serving time, remove and discard the top sheet of plastic wrap. Place a serving plate, facedown, over the loaf pan; invert the pan onto the plate. Lift the pan from the cake; remove and discard the remaining plastic wrap.
In a medium-sized bowl with a mixer at high speed, beat the remaining 1 cup cream, 1 tablespoon confectioners' sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla until stiff. Using a spatula, swirl decoratively over the cake. If desired, decorate with chocolate hearts and edible flowers; sprinkle lightly with additional confectioners' sugar. Makes 14 to 16 servings.
